mnemonics.title: Zombie Prevention
mnemonics.content: ZOMBIES: Zero Output Means Bad Inheritance Entry Stays - call wait() to clear the table entry

mnemonics.title: waitpid flags
mnemonics.content:
WNOHANG: Wait NOt HANG - return immediately if no child exited
WUNTRACED: Wait UNTRACED - return if child stopped
WCONTINUED: Wait CONTINUED - return if child resumed

acronyms.usage: Historical BSD signal sent when child process terminates
Standard POSIX signal for child termination notification, equivalent to SIGCLD in Linux
Flag to prevent SIGCHLD when child stops execution
Flag to automatically reap child processes without zombies
Flag for non-blocking wait in waitpid()

visualTips.visualization: Draw a process tree: Parent box connected to Child box. When Child terminates, draw ghost child with dashed border still attached to Parent. Add 'wait()' arrow from Parent to Child that removes the ghost.
Draw flow: Child exits → Kernel marks child as terminated → Kernel sends SIGCHLD to parent → Parent's handler executes → waitpid() called → Child entry removed from table → Handler returns.
Show normal case: Child → [Zombie box] → wait() → removed. With SA_NOCLDWAIT: Child → immediately disappears → reaped by init.

analogies.analogy: Like a student's name remaining on the attendance register after they've left the school until the principal (parent) marks them as 'collected'.
Like a postal worker delivering 'package arrived' notifications - the handler tells the parent 'a child finished, come collect it'.
Like a relay race where the middle runner (child) hands the baton (grandchild) and leaves the track before the second runner finishes - the race official (init) takes over.
Like an orphanage: when parents (parent process) die, children (child processes) are taken in by the orphanage (init process) which cares for them.
